images.altText: A person walks past a damaged building
People walk across the flattened remains of a bombed out apartment building with rubble strewn in all directions.
Emergency workers sitting on top of a truck parked by tall apartment towers partially destroyed by an air strike.

articles.title: Trump criticises NATO over Iran in meeting with alliance's boss

children.content: US President Donald Trump vents his frustration with NATO during a private meeting with its secretary-general as relations in the military alliance reach a crisis point over the Iran war.

image.altText: A close up of Mark Rutte, who wears a suit and glasses, against a blue background

articles.title: As Trump's rhetoric escalates, Albanese diplomatically eyes off-ramp

children.content: Neither side of politics in Australia rushed to enthusiastically celebrate the ceasefire deal that followed US President Donald Trump's threat.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese stands before a flag.

avatar.alt: David Speers sits on a couch in front of a yellow screen, smiling at the camera, in a suit and open-necked shirt.

articles.title: Iran issues US with ceasefire ultimatum

children.content: Israeli strikes on Lebanon threaten to scuttle the US-Iran ceasefire as Iranian state media reports the Strait of Hormuz has been closed.

image.altText: Donald Trump wearing a red "USA" hat, looking solemn as he walks at night.
Explosion in a city.
Smoke rises over a city skyline
Fire trucks surround the rubble of a building in the dark

children.content: Donald Trump says if an agreement is not reached, the "shootin' starts bigger and better and stronger than anyone has ever seen before". Follow live.

items.title: Trump says US military will remain in Middle East until an agreement is reached — or shooting will start again

updates.title: Iranian delegation to reach Islamabad Thursday
Trump says all US military to remain in region until agreement reached

items.title: High Court refuses to hear Bruce Lehrmann's appeal

children.content: The High Court was Bruce Lehrmann's last legal avenue to challenge a Federal Court ruling that found, on the balance of probabilities, he did rape Brittany Higgins.

image.altText: Bruce walks outside, flanked by others, wearing a blue suit, white shirt and pink tie and glasses.

items.title: What it was like inside Beirut as 100 strikes hit in 10 minutes

children.content: As a shaky ceasefire struggles to get under way, deadly Israeli strikes on Lebanon risk worsening the Middle East conflict.

image.altText: Groups of emergency workers spread around a destruction with burned cars and debris

items.title: Trump's threat against 'a whole civilisation' is coming back to haunt him

children.content: Donald Trump has survived what may have brought down many presidents before him. But the "end of civilisation" threat, as it's become known, has exposed a vulnerability he has not faced before.

image.altText: A close up of Donald Trump mid speech.

items.title: Is Hormuz open or closed? Shiptracking data reveals true picture

children.content: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has remained at an effective standstill in the 24 hours since Iran conditionally lifted its blockade on the critical shipping lane, amid a fragile ceasefire with the US and Israel.

image.altText: A satellite image of hundreds of ships floating in the ocean.

items.title: People living in vehicles forced to choose between fuel and food

children.content: The high cost of fuel is making it even more difficult for people living in their cars to afford food, electricity and transport.

articles.title: Trucks about to get longer, heavier in bid to reduce fuel costs

children.content: The South Australian government says recent changes to heavy vehicle freight limits will lower fuel costs and reduce the number of freight trucks needed on the road.

image.altText: an image of a car's driver side mirror with a truck reflected, driving through a town

articles.title: How long will it take petrol prices to get back to pre-Iran war levels, if at all?

children.content: Iran's promise to reopen the Strait of Hormuz after striking a temporary agreement with the US saw the cost of oil fall sharply. But how quickly are we likely to see relief at the bowser in Australia? Analysts say it could be weeks or months.

image.altText: Fuel bowser with a big truck in the background.

articles.title: How is the war in Iran impacting you or your business? Share with us

children.content: As fuel costs soar and the global economy reacts to a shortage of oil supplies, we need your help in identifying the impacts here in Australia. Whether it's the cost of your work commute or supply shortages affecting your business, share it with us.

image.altText: A row of petrol pumps.

gallery.altText: Anthony sits in a camp chair and reads a book outside his van.
Anthony sits in a camp chair outside his van. He has a cap, a beard and a worried expression.

thumbnail.altText: Anthony sits in a camp chair and reads a book outside his van.
Anthony sits in a camp chair outside his van. He has a cap, a beard and a worried expression.

avatar.alt: A headshot of ABC's Tony Ibrahim

items.title: Singapore's PM knows Australia is vulnerable but Albanese has leverage

children.content: Anthony Albanese is flying to Singapore as part of a hastily arranged regional mission to shore up crucial fuel supplies, amid warnings the global energy crisis could "sharply turn for the worse".

image.altText: Two men shake hands in front of two flags

items.title: China's 26 phone calls that brought on a US-Iran ceasefire

children.content: China's Foreign Minister Wang Yi says he held 26 phone calls with his counterparts in Iran, Israel, Russia and Gulf countries before the ceasefire was reached.

image.altText: A man holds up his index finger while talking.

items.title: Oil rallies as doubts about US-Iran ceasefire grow

children.content: Oil prices are higher following growing concerns the US and Iran's two-week ceasefire may now on the brink of collapse. Follow live.

image.altText: A satellite image of the Strait of Hormuz, and a inset zoomed in image of a large ship sailing in the ocean.

items.title: A wave of low-cost electric trucks is heading for Australia

children.content: Diesel trucks are the backbone of the national supply chain, but figures buried in recent sales data could mark the beginning of the end for these kings of the road.

image.altText: An electric prime-mover plugged in to recharge

items.title: Top neuroscientists slam AFL move to back new headgear

children.content: The AFL has backed a new helmet that aims to reduce the risk of concussion, but a top neuroscientist is calling it "bad scientific practice".

gallery.altText: five helmets placed on a sporting field
A rugby player wearing a padded black helmet looks to pass the ball.
A black padded sports helmet on a white background.

thumbnail.altText: five helmets placed on a sporting field
A rugby player wearing a padded black helmet looks to pass the ball.
A black padded sports helmet on a white background.

items.title: Uni in financial crisis spent nearly $1.5m on leadership coach, documents show

children.content: Documents released to Four Corners show the University of Technology Sydney spent hundreds of thousands of dollars a year on a leadership coach before planning to slash jobs and courses to save money.

image.altText: A person carrying a bag walks through a university campus. Behind them is a large multi-story glass building.

items.title: First glimpse of $600m Sydney Metro West rail designs

children.content: The NSW government says the transport hubs will connect commuters from western areas of Sydney to the CBD in "under 20 minutes".

image.altText: A building with a white angular roof with an 'M' sign with a few trees and gardens around it.

items.title: Large fire damages roof of Olympic velodrome

children.content: Rio de Janeiro's Olympic velodrome has been damaged following a fire, which required about 80 firefighters and 20 fire trucks to bring under control.

image.altText: An aerial view of a large stadium that is an oval shape, there is a glow of orange fire on its rooftop f

gallery.altText: Flames and smoke rise from a fire on the roof of a large arena

thumbnail.altText: Flames and smoke rise from a fire on the roof of a large arena

items.title: Man accused of failing to help his dying wife back behind bars

children.content: Kevin Stanischewski is accused of leaving his wife unconscious for hours after she allegedly told him she had drunk poison.

image.altText: A white, modernist, flat-fronted building with "supreme court" in capital letters above a wide entrance.

items.title: 'I've got my visa back': Child sex offender allowed to stay in Australia

children.content: Edward James Kilbey's permanent visa was automatically cancelled when he was jailed in 2024 for sexually abusing a nine-year-old girl, but a tribunal has upheld the 83-year-old's appeal in part due to his "ill health".

image.altText: A man wearing sunglasses on a swing at a children's playground.

items.title: Comedian Grace Jarvis on most bizarre encounter working in sex shop

children.content: Grace Jarvis talks about everything from her experiences with autism, ADHD, queerness, and working in a sex shop in her stand-up. Her experiences with chronic illness? Not so much.

articles.title: Test your knowledge with our classic Australian TV quiz

children.content: One quote, two possible answers: test your knowledge and take a nostalgic trip down memory lane as you match the quote to the iconic Australian television series.

image.altText: Ray Meagher on the set of Home and Away playing Alf Stewart.

articles.title: Failed referendum inspires two top dance companies to join forces

children.content: Sixteen dancers from Bangarra Dance Theatre and 19 ballet dancers from the Australian Ballet have joined forces for Flora, choreographed by Bangarra artistic director Frances Rings.

image.altText: On stage, an Indigenous dancer leans back, stretching one arm up. Behind her other dancers in shadow form a line.

gallery.altText: Grace Jarvis smiles while holding a mic and talking on stage.

thumbnail.altText: Grace Jarvis smiles while holding a mic and talking on stage.

items.title: Four holes-in-one as 8yo steals show on Masters eve

children.content: The annual Par 3 Contest on the eve of the US Masters has produced four aces, but Tommy Fleetwood's eight-year-old son stole the show at Augusta National.

image.altText: Tommy Fleetwood with his son and Northern Ireland's Rory McIlroy's daughter Poppy

items.title: Tigers searching for third straight win to end eight-year streak

children.content: The last time Wests Tigers won three matches in a row, Ivan Cleary was still the club's coach and Benji Marshall had just begun his comeback as a player. Now, after eight years of waiting, they have the chance to end that streak.

image.altText: Wests Tigers celebrate after winning an NRL match

items.title: Woman charged after man allegedly struck with hammer in Sydney hospital

children.content: A woman has been charged after allegedly attacking a man with a hammer at a Sydney hospital overnight.

image.altText: A purple and yellow sign says "Welcome to RPA" with arrows pointing to "Main Entrance" and "Emergency".

items.title: $1m reward in 2003 cold-case death of Harmony Bryant

children.content: Police hope a $1 million reward will produce fresh information about the death of a 26-year-old Kyogle woman, who was found suffering severe burns in bushland on the NSW Mid North Coast.

image.altText: An image of a young woman, smiling with long brown hair.

items.title: $10m horse will never race, but owner insists she is worth every cent

children.content: An owner says she has "no regrets" after announcing that the first filly from champion race mare Winx will never race.

gallery.altText: A smiling man with a foal and her mothert, both with dark coats, on a ccountry property.

thumbnail.altText: A smiling man with a foal and her mothert, both with dark coats, on a ccountry property.

avatar.alt: Amelia Bernasconi, wearing a bright pink jacket, leaning on a fence post.

items.title: Hundreds more PSOs at Melbourne stations if Liberals win state election

children.content: The Victorian opposition is promising to recruit hundreds more protective service officers if it wins government in November.

image.altText: Three protective service officers wearing masks walk down a footpath in Melbourne's CBD.

items.title: 'Preferred profit … over the environment': Farm owner fined for clearing 112 hectares of bushland

children.content: The owner of a farm in South Australia's south-east is convicted and fined for clearing 112 hectares of bushland, but the penalty is much less than the $2.2 million he would have had to pay for a permit for the work.

image.altText: A small bird with grey and gold plumage with a black chin

items.title: Homicide investigation launched in Perth's north after 69yo man dies

children.content: A man dies in hospital after an altercation at a home in Padbury, and police say a 39-year-old man arrested at the scene is assisting investigators with their inquiries.

image.altText: Police present at a crime scene.

items.title: Four charged for alleged offences 'connected' to murder of Adelaide father Chris Robertson

children.content: Four people have been arrested by police for alleged offences "connected" to the alleged murder of Adelaide father-of-two Chris Robertson last year.

image.altText: A photo of Chris Robertson whose death is being treated as murder.

items.title: Couple forced to move home accidentally built on wrong block

children.content: A Victorian couple has been forced to move their home that was accidentally built on the wrong block.

gallery.altText: a man and a woman look at the camera with a serious look on their face
house in a field with a blue sky
a home is stacked on oil drums for support

thumbnail.altText: a man and a woman look at the camera with a serious look on their face
house in a field with a blue sky
a home is stacked on oil drums for support

items.title: Playwright who won $250k proud to bring Aussie stories to global stage

children.content: S Shakthidharan, whose plays include The Wrong Gods, The Jungle and the Sea and Counting and Cracking, has won the prestigious Windham-Campbell Prize.

image.altText: S Shakthidharan, a Sri Lankan Australian man, then in his late 30s, smiles brightly, arms folded, in a rehearsal room.

items.title: Nitrous oxide a 'significant factor' in Canberra teen's drowning death

children.content: An 18-year-old was heavily impaired by nitrous oxide, known as "nangs", when his car entered a pond, prompting a coroner to call for a review of the gas's availability in the ACT.

image.altText: A pond with two road bridges going over it.

items.title: Brisbane suburbs set to become 'mini CBDs' as city plans for population surge

children.content: As Brisbane prepares for an expected surge in population over the next 15 years, the council has introduced a plan to allow high-rise apartments in some of the city's suburban hubs.

image.altText: A sand coloured block of apartments

items.title: House empty more than a year after 69-year-old renter forced out

children.content: After a lifetime of caring for the elderly, Linda Braam is left battling for a place to call home.

image.altText: An older woman in glasses looks sternly at the camera, as she holds the handle of the front door to a house.

items.title: US architect admits killing eight women in Gilgo Beach murders

children.content: Rex Heuermann pleads guilty to seven murders and admits to killing another woman, solving the notorious case known as the Gilgo Beach killings.

gallery.altText: A man wearing a suit and tie smiling in a court room.
Crime scene investigators putting on full-body PPE out the front of a home.
A wide shot of a sandy beach featuring an American flag on a pole, with the ocean in the background.

thumbnail.altText: A man wearing a suit and tie smiling in a court room.
Crime scene investigators putting on full-body PPE out the front of a home.
A wide shot of a sandy beach featuring an American flag on a pole, with the ocean in the background.

items.title: Remote community's bid to relocate progresses after '30-odd years of asking'

children.content: Flood-affected residents from Daly River are in talks with the NT government about moving the remote Aboriginal community to higher ground, with a church offering to donate land for the township's relocation.

image.altText: An aerial image shows a swollen river on the edge of a remote community, with brown water flooding local homes.

items.title: Unusable road may be the end of remote community as food supplies dwindle

children.content: A remote Aboriginal community in Western Australia has six weeks' worth of food remaining and no truck driver is willing to travel down the notorious road to reach it.

image.altText: A damaged road with red sand on either side.
